bias_defense_notes: 6 MCQs shuffled. Position counts: pos0=1 (17%), pos1=1 (17%), pos2=2 (33%), pos3=2 (33%). max_share=33% ≤ 40%. All 4 positions used. Both post-shuffle constraint checks passed. q7 is short-answer; excluded from distribution.

questions.objective_ref: Define a constraint in the context of prompt engineering and explain how it differs from a positive instruction.
Identify the four main types of constraints — format, tone, content exclusion, and scope limit — and give one example of each.
Apply the positive-reframe technique to convert a vague negative instruction into a clear, model-friendly constraint.
Explain why models sometimes ignore or misapply negated instructions, and choose placement strategies that reduce this risk.
Recognise common constraint pitfalls — vague negations, overloading, and contradictions — and describe what goes wrong in each case.
Know where constraints belong: system prompt (persistent) vs user message (single-turn).
Apply the positive-reframe technique to convert a vague negative instruction into a clear, model-friendly constraint.
